# Gravityzone Tools

## Overview

gSage AI ‚Äî GravityZone Endpoints tool.

## Tools in this group

| Tool | Summary | Permissions | Requires approval | Core |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | :---: |
| `gz_endpoints` | Query GravityZone network inventory for endpoint details, managed devices, and installation info | `gravityzone:read` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `gz_management` | Execute write operations in GravityZone: isolate/restore endpoints, update blocklist entries | `gravityzone:write` | ‚úì | ‚Äî |
| `gz_security` | Read GravityZone security posture data: endpoint blocklist and PHASR policy recommendations | `gravityzone:read` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

## Configuration

> The JSON below goes into the **configtool model** in the web UI (per organisation; can also be scoped to a department).

> **Sensitive fields** (api keys / passwords / tokens) are *omitted* from the JSON below ‚Äî set them via environment variables (see the _Environment variables_ table) or directly in the encrypted DB row from the web UI.

### Empty configtool template (required fields only)

```json
{}
```

### Example configtool model

```json
{
  "api_key": "<value>",
  "base_url": "https://cloud.gravityzone.bitdefender.com/api"
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `api_key` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚úì | ‚Äî | GravityZone API key (from Control Center ‚Üí My Account ‚Üí API keys). |
| `base_url` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `"https://cloud.gravityzone.bitdefender.com/api"` | GravityZone API base URL.  Default: https://cloud.gravityzone.bitdefender.com/api.  Override for on-premise deployments. |

## Environment variables

The variables below are derived automatically from each tool's `config_schema`. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) to seed defaults for every organisation. **Per-org DB config takes precedence over env vars.**

| Variable | Maps to field | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TOOL_GRAVITYZONE__API_KEY` | `api_key` | `string` | ‚Äî | ‚úì | GravityZone API key (from Control Center ‚Üí My Account ‚Üí API keys). |
| `TOOL_GRAVITYZONE__BASE_URL` | `base_url` | `string` | `"https://cloud.gravityzone.bitdefender.com/api"` | ‚Äî | GravityZone API base URL.  Default: https://cloud.gravityzone.bitdefender.com/api.  Override for on-premise deployments. |
| `TOOL_GZ_MANAGEMENT__AUTO_APPROVE` | `auto_approve` | `boolean` | `false` | ‚Äî | When true, HITL approval requests for this tool are auto-approved by the backend without human review. Useful for lab/dev environments. DB toolconfig overrides env TOOL_<NAME>__AUTO_APPROVE; default is false. |

_Note: any field above can also be overridden per-tool by using the prefix `TOOL_<TOOL_NAME>__` instead of the shared `TOOL_<NAMESPACE>__` ‚Äî useful when a single tool in the family needs a distinct value._

## Operations

### `gz_endpoints`

- **`list`** ‚Äî paginate endpoints (supports filters, up to 1000/page via API v1.1)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `is_managed` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Filter by managed status (action=list only).  true=only managed, false=only unmanaged, omit=all. |
  | `page` | `integer` | ‚Äî | First page to retrieve (action=list, default: 1). |
  | `max_pages` | `integer` | ‚Äî | Maximum pages to fetch for action=list (default: 5). |
  | `export_csv` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Persist all fetched endpoints as a CSV artifact (action=list only). PREFER CSV when the user asks to 'save', 'export' or 'download' the endpoint inventory ‚Äî it is the natural format for tabular data and easier to open in spreadsheets. CSV is also generated automatically when the result exceeds 100 rows so the user always has a way to access the full data. |
  | `export_json` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Persist all fetched endpoints as a JSON artifact (action=list only). Use only when the user explicitly asks for JSON or needs the file for programmatic post-processing ‚Äî otherwise prefer 'export_csv'. |
  | `resolve_group_names` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Resolve groupId ‚Üí group name (best-effort) for action=list. Adds one extra RPC call per top-level group; falls back silently on permission errors. |
  | `group_by` | `array` | ‚Äî | Optional list of (normalised) column names to use for top-N analytics on action=list. When omitted, a sensible default set is chosen (machine_type, os_version, group_name, ...). |

- **`details`** ‚Äî full record for one endpoint_id

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `endpoint_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Endpoint object ID.  Required for action=details. |


### `gz_management`

- **`add_to_blocklist`** ‚Äî add hash/path/connection rules (requires blocklist_type, rules)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `blocklist_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | Type of blocklist rule to add. Required for add_to_blocklist. - hash: block by file hash (MD5 or SHA256) - path: block by file path - connection: block by network connection details |
  | `rules` | `array` | ‚Äî | Array of rule objects to add to the blocklist (add_to_blocklist).  For blocklist_type=hash, each rule requires:   { "algorithm": "sha256" \| "md5", "hash": "<hex>", "note": "<reason>" }  For blocklist_type=path, each rule requires:   { "path": "<file_path>", "note": "<reason>" }  For blocklist_type=connection, each rule requires:   { "rule_name": "<name>", "command_line": "<cmd>",     "protocol": "<tcp\|udp>", "direction": "<in\|out\|both>",     "ip_version": "<4\|6>", "local_address": "<cidr>",     "remote_address": "<cidr>", "operating_system": "<os>" } |
  | `recursive` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Apply blocklist rule recursively to sub-companies (add_to_blocklist, default: true). |

- **`remove_from_blocklist`** ‚Äî remove entries by ID (requires blocklist_item_ids)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `blocklist_item_ids` | `array` | ‚Äî | Array of blocklist entry IDs to remove. Required for remove_from_blocklist. Obtain IDs from gz_security action=blocklist_items. |

- **`isolate_endpoint`** ‚Äî isolate endpoint from network (requires endpoint_id)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `endpoint_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Endpoint ID. Required for isolate_endpoint and restore_isolation. |

- **`restore_isolation`** ‚Äî restore isolated endpoint (requires endpoint_id)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `endpoint_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Endpoint ID. Required for isolate_endpoint and restore_isolation. |

- **`change_incident_status`** ‚Äî update status (requires incident_type, incident_id, status)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `incident_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | Incident API namespace. Required for change_incident_status and update_incident_note. - incidents: standard security incidents - extendedIncidents: XDR/advanced incidents |
  | `incident_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Incident ID. Required for change_incident_status and update_incident_note. |
  | `status` | `string` | ‚Äî | New incident status. Required for change_incident_status. |

- **`update_incident_note`** ‚Äî add/update note (requires incident_type, incident_id, note)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `incident_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | Incident API namespace. Required for change_incident_status and update_incident_note. - incidents: standard security incidents - extendedIncidents: XDR/advanced incidents |
  | `incident_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Incident ID. Required for change_incident_status and update_incident_note. |
  | `note` | `string` | ‚Äî | Note text to attach to the incident (max 50 000 chars). Required for update_incident_note. |


### `gz_security`

- **`blocklist_items`** ‚Äî list all blocklist entries (hash/path/connection)

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `company_id` | `string` | ‚Äî | Company (organization) ID.  Required for all phasr_* actions. |
  | `search_string` | `string` | ‚Äî | Search/filter string for phasr_resources and phasr_identities. |
  | `categories` | `array` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by threat category (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: tampering_tool, hack_tool, remote_tool, miner, lol_bin. |
  | `action_taken` | `array` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by applied status (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: action_needed, applied, partially_applied. |
  | `recommendation_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by type (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: allow_access, restrict_access, allow_access_request. |
  | `sort` | `string` | ‚Äî | Sort PHASR recommendations by field (default: created_on). |
  | `dir` | `string` | ‚Äî | Sort direction for PHASR recommendations (default: DESC). |
  | `page` | `integer` | ‚Äî | First page to fetch (default: 1). With max_pages > 1, successive pages are walked starting from this offset. |
  | `per_page` | `integer` | ‚Äî | Items per page (max 100, default: 30). |
  | `max_pages` | `integer` | ‚Äî | Maximum pages to fetch (default: 1 ‚Äî single page). Increase to auto-paginate up to per_page √ó max_pages records. The shared overflow logic still caps the inline preview at 100 rows. |
  | `export_csv` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Persist all fetched rows as a CSV artifact. PREFER CSV when the user asks to 'save', 'export' or 'download' the results ‚Äî it is the natural format for tabular data. CSV is also generated automatically when more than 100 rows are returned. |
  | `export_json` | `boolean` | ‚Äî | Persist all fetched rows as a JSON artifact. Use only when the user explicitly asks for JSON ‚Äî otherwise prefer 'export_csv'. |
  | `group_by` | `array` | ‚Äî | Optional list of column names for top-N analytics. When omitted, an action-specific default set is used. |
  | `top_n` | `integer` | ‚Äî | Top-N size for each grouped column (default: 10). |

- **`phasr_recommendations`** ‚Äî PHASR recommendations for a company

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `categories` | `array` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by threat category (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: tampering_tool, hack_tool, remote_tool, miner, lol_bin. |
  | `action_taken` | `array` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by applied status (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: action_needed, applied, partially_applied. |
  | `recommendation_type` | `string` | ‚Äî | Filter PHASR recommendations by type (action=phasr_recommendations only).  Values: allow_access, restrict_access, allow_access_request. |

- **`phasr_resources`** ‚Äî behavioral profile resources for a company

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `search_string` | `string` | ‚Äî | Search/filter string for phasr_resources and phasr_identities. |

- **`phasr_identities`** ‚Äî behavioral profile identities for a company

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `search_string` | `string` | ‚Äî | Search/filter string for phasr_resources and phasr_identities. |
